name: OpenZeppelin
description: OpenZeppelin — Security library and auditing framework for Solidity smart contracts with battle-tested reusable contract modules.

seo.title: OpenZeppelin — Security Scanning / Mobile App Sec
seo.meta_description: OpenZeppelin: OpenZeppelin — Security library and auditing framework for Solidity smart contracts with battle-tested reusable contract modules.

when_to_use: Audited ERC-20 and ERC-721 contract modules for Solidity devs
Access control library for role-based smart contract permissions
Upgradeable proxy pattern for protocol governance changes
Security audit framework for DeFi protocol pre-launch review
OpenZeppelin Defender for automated contract operations

pros: Battle-tested contract library is the Solidity standard
Free, open source, and actively maintained
Defender toolkit adds ops, monitoring, and upgrades
Strong audit team with deep protocol experience
cons: Upgradeable contract patterns add their own risks
Audit queue and pricing favor larger protocols
Defender advanced features require paid plans
Heavy reliance creates homogeneous bug surface across DeFi
review.verdict: The default Solidity security stack; hard to justify rolling your own primitives against it.
review.best_for: EVM protocol teams building production smart contracts who want proven primitives and tooling.
review.not_for: Non-EVM chains or teams allergic to open-source licensing obligations in Defender.
